stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the highway route) yields a guaranteed return of $136K. Option B (the scenic route) depends on traffic conditions: with probability 44-67% (uncertain), it yields $331K; otherwise it yields $48K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in traffic conditions affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $136K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $172.5K to $237.6K. Point estimate: $206.5K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 31.1%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 31.1%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
